source_alignment.subject: English
source_alignment.topic: Year 2 spelling patterns and suffix choices
source_alignment.source_statement: Learn new ways of spelling phonemes for which one or more spellings are already known, and apply Year 2 spelling rules and guidance.
source_alignment.derived_objective_note: This pack teaches pattern noticing, word sorting and suffix decisions. Whole-word and phoneme-sensitive audio must use produced specialist-reviewed recordings; browser TTS is prohibited.

objective.child_goal: I can notice a spelling pattern and use it to build a word.
objective.statement: Recognise, explain and apply taught Year 2 grapheme and suffix patterns in familiar words.
misconceptions: Chooses a spelling only because it sounds right, without checking the taught pattern.
Treats every word with the same sound as having the same grapheme.
Adds a suffix without making a required base-word change.
Relies on a picture or word shape instead of checking graphemes in order.

vocabulary.child_explanation: A letter pattern that appears in a group of words.
vocabulary.audio_script: A spelling pattern is a useful letter pattern we can notice and reuse.
vocabulary.visual_anchor: Matching grapheme tiles highlighted across word cards.

vocabulary.child_explanation: A letter or group of letters that writes a sound.
vocabulary.audio_script: A grapheme is how we write a phoneme.
vocabulary.visual_anchor: One sound counter replaced by one or more linked letter tiles.

vocabulary.child_explanation: The word before we add an ending.
vocabulary.audio_script: The base word carries the main meaning before a suffix is added.
vocabulary.visual_anchor: A root word tile with a separate suffix tile.

vocabulary.child_explanation: Letters added to the end of a word.
vocabulary.audio_script: A suffix is added at the end and can change the word.
vocabulary.visual_anchor: A small ending tile snapping after a base word.

teaching_sequence.child_prompt: Listen to the two words. What sound do they share?
teaching_sequence.learning_purpose: Attend to the target sound before seeing spelling choices.
teaching_sequence.audio_script: Listen to both whole words. Hold the shared sound in your mind.
teaching_sequence.visual_model: Two covered word cards with adult-recorded playback controls.

teaching_sequence.child_prompt: Reveal the words and circle the letters that spell the shared sound.
teaching_sequence.learning_purpose: Connect a heard phoneme to an explicitly taught grapheme pattern.
teaching_sequence.audio_script: Now look closely. Which letters are doing the sound-writing job?
teaching_sequence.visual_model: Word cards reveal and the target graphemes receive identical underlines.

teaching_sequence.child_prompt: The same sound can be written in more than one way. Sort each word by its grapheme.
teaching_sequence.learning_purpose: Prevent overgeneralisation by comparing teacher-approved contrasting word sets.
teaching_sequence.audio_script: Listen, then check the letters. We learn which spelling belongs in each word.
teaching_sequence.visual_model: Pattern columns receive whole-word cards, not isolated rule guesses.

teaching_sequence.child_prompt: Watch happy change to happier. What changed before we added er?
teaching_sequence.learning_purpose: Model a Year 2 suffix transformation while preserving the base word's meaning.
teaching_sequence.audio_script: Happy ends consonant y. Change y to i, then add er: happier.
teaching_sequence.visual_model: The final y tile turns to i before the suffix tile joins.

teaching_sequence.child_prompt: Listen to the word, choose its pattern, build it, then compare every grapheme.
teaching_sequence.learning_purpose: Check an SSP-aligned listen-segment-build-check routine without handwriting or speech-scoring barriers.
teaching_sequence.audio_script: Use the produced recording. Build left to right and check each part.
teaching_sequence.visual_model: Sound boxes, taught grapheme tiles and a covered answer card.

teaching_sequence.child_prompt: Teach Pip why we cannot use browser speech or guess any spelling that sounds the same.
teaching_sequence.learning_purpose: Confirm that pronunciation needs specialist recordings and spelling requires word-specific pattern knowledge.
teaching_sequence.audio_script: How do I listen safely and then check the spelling evidence?
teaching_sequence.visual_model: Pip chooses between a gated human recording and a disabled browser-TTS button, then checks grapheme tiles.

manipulatives.purpose: Compare whole words and group them by the grapheme representing a shared phoneme.
manipulatives.interaction_notes: Children play named recordings, then tap, drag, keyboard-step or switch-scan word cards into labelled pattern columns; a reveal mode underlines the decisive grapheme.
manipulatives.accessibility_notes: No browser TTS, no automatic speech scoring, large cards, spoken labels from approved assets only, colour-independent underlines, and adult-led mode when audio assets are not yet approved.

manipulatives.purpose: Make base-word changes visible before a suffix is attached.
manipulatives.interaction_notes: Children choose a base word, perform one explicit tile change if needed, then add a suffix; undo and compare preserve each intermediate state.
manipulatives.accessibility_notes: Single-tap alternatives replace dragging, each transformation is announced and captioned, reduced-motion uses three static panels, and handwriting is never the sole response.

practice.interleaving_notes: Variant release must follow the adopted SSP and Year 2 spelling sequence. Introduce one reviewed contrast at a time, then interleave previously taught graphemes and suffix rules.

variant_blueprints.purpose: Match produced whole-word recordings to explicitly taught grapheme patterns.
variant_blueprints.generation_pattern: two reviewed pattern columns + 4-6 familiar words + target grapheme reveal
variant_blueprints.review_notes: Every recording and word set requires SSP-specialist review for accent, articulation and progression.

variant_blueprints.purpose: Compare words sharing a phoneme but using different known graphemes.
variant_blueprints.generation_pattern: specialist recording pairs + grapheme evidence + no invented pronunciation rule
variant_blueprints.review_notes: Do not imply a universal positional rule where English spelling is lexical or programme-dependent.

variant_blueprints.purpose: Build familiar words from taught graphemes after listening and segmenting.
variant_blueprints.generation_pattern: approved whole-word asset + sound boxes + taught tiles + plausible reviewed distractors
variant_blueprints.review_notes: Pure phoneme clips remain gated until separately recorded and specialist-listened; browser TTS is forbidden.

variant_blueprints.purpose: Apply reviewed rules for endings such as -ment, -ness, -ful, -less, -ly, -er and -est.
variant_blueprints.generation_pattern: base word + suffix choice + required keep/change step + explanation
variant_blueprints.review_notes: Scope each item to the statutory pattern and avoid untaught morphology.

variant_blueprints.purpose: Retain taught spelling patterns across listening, sorting and building forms.
variant_blueprints.generation_pattern: SSP-tagged review queue + approved audio + one known contrast + grapheme-by-grapheme check
variant_blueprints.review_notes: Exclude browser TTS, speed scoring, microphone requirements and untaught correspondences.

body.prompt: Listen to each whole word, then sort by the letters spelling the target sound.

hints: Listen to the complete word first.
Then look at the letters writing the shared sound.
question_variants.explanation: These words share a phoneme, but the reviewed spellings use dge in badge and bridge and g in giant and magic.

body.prompt: Listen to the approved recording of 'knock'. Build the word with the taught tiles.

hints: Use the taught grapheme at the start.
Check the final grapheme after the vowel.
question_variants.explanation: The reviewed spelling is knock: kn writes the first phoneme and ck writes the final phoneme.

expected_answer.transformation: change y to i, then add er

hints: Look at the consonant before the final y.
Change y to i before adding er.
question_variants.explanation: For happy, change the final y to i and add er to make happier.

body.prompt: Which action gives safe evidence for the spelling?
choices: Use the specialist-approved whole-word recording and check the graphemes
Ask browser TTS to invent the pronunciation

expected_answer.value: Use the specialist-approved whole-word recording and check the graphemes

hints: Spelling evidence is in the graphemes.
question_variants.explanation: Specialist-reviewed audio supports listening, and the ordered graphemes provide the spelling evidence.

misconception_repairs.misconception: Chooses a spelling only because it sounds right, without checking the taught pattern.
misconception_repairs.detect_with: The child selects any homophonically plausible tile sequence and ignores the word-specific grapheme.
repair_sequence: Underline and rebuild the target grapheme in order.
misconception_repairs.evidence_of_repair: The child identifies the decisive grapheme in a new reviewed word.

misconception_repairs.misconception: Treats every word with the same sound as having the same grapheme.
misconception_repairs.detect_with: The child sorts all shared-phoneme words into one spelling column.
repair_sequence: Reveal graphemes on two contrasting word cards.
Sort a new pair using visible letter evidence.
misconception_repairs.evidence_of_repair: The child sorts a new reviewed contrast and explains that one phoneme can have different spellings.

misconception_repairs.misconception: Adds a suffix without making a required base-word change.
misconception_repairs.detect_with: The child produces forms such as happyer.
repair_sequence: Separate base word and suffix tiles.
Complete the change step before attaching the suffix.
misconception_repairs.evidence_of_repair: The child performs and explains the required change for a new base word.

misconception_repairs.misconception: Relies on a picture or word shape instead of checking graphemes in order.
misconception_repairs.detect_with: The child chooses a visually similar distractor without reading the tile sequence.
repair_sequence: Hide the picture after confirming meaning.
Track sound boxes left to right.
Compare each chosen grapheme with the revealed spelling.
misconception_repairs.evidence_of_repair: The child builds a new word in grapheme order without picture matching.

adaptive_support.low_sensory: Use plain word cards, no background music under speech, muted confirmation and no bouncing letters.
adaptive_support.reduced_motion: Show static listen-sort-check and base-change-suffix panels.
adaptive_support.audio_first: Use produced whole-word recordings only after specialist listening approval; pure phoneme clips are separately gated and browser TTS is never used.
adaptive_support.reading_support: Show one word per row, generous spacing, grapheme underlines and taught tiles only.
adaptive_support.attention_support: Use one target contrast per screen and a consistent listen-look-build-check routine.
adaptive_support.processing_support: Allow unlimited replay of approved assets, slow adult-led modelling, pause between transformations and undo.
adaptive_support.confidence_support: Accept tile placement, pointing or adult observation; do not require microphone input or public spelling speed.
adaptive_support.eal_support: Confirm word meaning with a picture first, then remove picture cues; never mark accent differences as spelling failure.

evidence.mastery_rule: Expected requires accurate application of taught patterns in reviewed familiar words; secure requires retained contrast and suffix reasoning across formats with approved audio assets.
evidence.parent_summary: Can notice taught spelling patterns, build familiar words and make simple base-word changes before adding suffixes.
evidence.teacher_evidence: Uses specialist-approved whole-word audio, identifies grapheme evidence, builds in order and explains a taught suffix transformation without browser TTS or speech scoring.
next_actions: Map every variant to the school's SSP and Year 2 spelling sequence.
Commission and specialist-review named whole-word and phoneme-sensitive recordings.
Separate handwriting practice so motor demands do not mask spelling knowledge.

qa.notes: Deep review-status Year 2 wave pack. Browser TTS is prohibited. Pilot is blocked pending SSP/programme mapping, produced specialist recordings, SSP-specialist listening approval, independent teacher review, SEND testing and renderer evidence.
